failure_cause.summary: demos/edgeguard/reset_demo.sh invoked a hard-coded python3 interpreter inside a subprocess while pytest was running under the project virtualenv.

evidence.detail: Edgeguard reset subprocess used python3 and failed with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'cryptography'.

fix_summary: reset_demo.sh now resolves the Edgeguard reset interpreter from USBAY_PYTHON, then VIRTUAL_ENV/bin/python, then python3.
reset_demo.sh fails closed when the resolved interpreter is unavailable.
reset_demo.sh records the resolved python_executable in every signed reset audit entry.
Edgeguard tests now pass USBAY_PYTHON=sys.executable to reset subprocesses.
A regression test verifies reset audit entries use the same Python interpreter as pytest.

commands_run.output_summary: Default python3 lacked pytest, confirming interpreter mismatch risk.
Confirmed project virtualenv has cryptography 47.0.0 and pytest 9.0.3.
Full suite was attempted. The stale runner did not complete cleanly in the sandbox and earlier non-Edgeguard failures were observed. This cannot be treated as merge-ready evidence.

full_pytest.reason: Full suite did not produce a clean completion result in this run.

remaining_gaps.description: Full pytest did not complete with a clean pass result during this repair validation.
remaining_gaps.required_closure: Rerun the full suite in a controlled environment and record a clean pass, or triage every remaining non-Edgeguard failure with explicit evidence.

merge_readiness.reason: Targeted Edgeguard failure is fixed, but full-suite validation remains incomplete and therefore fails closed.
